title: posoqueria latifolia subsp. gracilis
intro: Posoqueria latifolia subsp. gracilis, commonly known as the Posoqueria or Gracilis Posoqueria, is a flowering plant species within the Rubiaceae family, native to Mexico and Central America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever. The Gracilis Posoqueria is rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to its health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as antioxidant effects that may help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Posoqueria latifolia subsp. gracilis can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and alleviate symptoms of fever and infection.
